sightseeings.title: Software change with 2nd pair of eyes

sightseeings.details:
- You change it, you test it
- You change it, someone else reviews and tests it

sightseeings.summary: Information to enrich with testing

- Feature (a scoped group of changes of value to user)
- Individual changes

sightseeings.summary: When we stop talking about artifacts...
sightseeings.details:
- learning the domain/application fast towards thorough
- doing testing, designing experiments
- learning for purposes of doing testing (from plans and from work done)
- enabling others to learn through testing
- setting up enablers for testing (env, data, tools)
- targeting testing (strategy, coverage)
- monitoring across engineering environments
- estimation of necessary talent availability
- agreeing on logistics

sightseeings.title: Value and waste
sightseeings.summary: Forms of lean waste in testing
sightseeings.details:
- Forms of lean waste, and how the way we organize for testing contributes
- Forms of lean value, and how the way we collaborate enables it

sightseeings.title: Testing, debugging and fixing
sightseeings.summary: The division of knowledge without task expansion
sightseeings.details:
- Testing
- Debugging
- Fixing

sightseeings.title: Domain expertise
sightseeings.summary: Recognizing incomplete beyond specifications
sightseeings.details:
- Learning the domain for testing
- Useful specifications over complete specifications
- Specs as socializing knowledge

sightseeings.title: Tacit and implicit knowledge
sightseeings.summary: Sustaining information when people change

sightseeings.title: Artifacts vs. performance
sightseeings.summary: Results from performance, evolving artifacts
sightseeings.details:
- Evolving artifacts
- Results from performance

title: Git-first mindset
summary: Version control as the backbone of all work

sightseeings.title: Commits & PRs
sightseeings.summary: Atomic commits, meaningful messages, pull request etiquette
sightseeings.details:
A git-first mindset starts with understanding commits as snapshots of intent, not just saves.

• Write commits that tell a story — each one should answer "why"
• Open pull requests as a communication tool, not just a merge mechanism
• Review PRs as collaborative quality gates, not gatekeeping
• Use draft PRs to share work-in-progress and gather early feedback

sightseeings.title: Cloning & Forking
sightseeings.summary: Working with repositories locally and contributing upstream
sightseeings.details:
Understanding the difference between cloning and forking unlocks how open-source and inner-source collaboration works.

• Clone: get a full local copy of a repo you have direct access to
• Fork: create your own copy of someone else's repo to propose changes
• Set upstream remotes to stay in sync with the origin
• Know when to branch vs. when to fork

sightseeings.title: Merge Strategies
sightseeings.summary: Merge commits, squash, rebase — choosing the right strategy
sightseeings.details:
How you integrate changes shapes the history and the team's ability to understand evolution.

• Merge commit: preserves full branch history, clear but can be noisy
• Squash merge: collapses a branch into one clean commit on main
• Rebase: rewrites commits on top of main — linear history, requires discipline
• Understand fast-forward vs. three-way merges
• Team conventions matter more than the "best" strategy

sightseeings.title: Git hygiene: secrets, dependencies and gitignore
sightseeings.summary: Managing your environment
sightseeings.details:
- .gitignore templates for common languages and frameworks
- Never commit secrets: API keys, passwords, tokens, .env files
- Use pre-commit hooks (git-secrets, trufflehog) to catch secrets before they land
- Dependency lock files: commit them, they pin your reproducible build
- Reviewing staged changes before committing: git diff --staged
- Rotating credentials immediately when they are accidentally committed

title: Permissions and limitations
summary: Acknowledge risks and work within agreements

sightseeings.title: From work questions to specs and code to data in envs and secrets
sightseeings.summary: Allowances have limits
sightseeings.details:
- Work items and task descriptions: what you're allowed to access and share
- Specifications and code: IP ownership, confidentiality, and client agreements
- Data in environments: personal data, production data, client data under contract
- Secrets in environment variables: who can see them, when they rotate
- What leaves your laptop: knowing which tools send data to external services
- AI tools and data governance: checking before pasting client code into a chatbot

sightseeings.title: Data classification in practice with licenses
sightseeings.summary: Handing data over under a contract
sightseeings.details:
- Public, internal, confidential, restricted: how classification limits tooling choices
- GDPR and data residency: where can test data live?
- Anonymization and pseudonymization for using production-like test data
- Software licenses: MIT, GPL, Apache, proprietary — what each allows
- License compatibility when combining open source components
- Reading a contract: what does 'you may use this for testing' actually mean

sightseeings.title: Permissions and permanent agreements
sightseeings.summary: Changes in mutual behaviors.

title: Task expansion with AI
summary: Ability to do things that you used to need a colleague for.

sightseeings.summary: Call it unit or functional, or small. But leave it behind.

sightseeings.title: Shift-left: socializing requirements
sightseeings.summary: Understanding over specifying
sightseeings.details:
- Documentation for people and for AI

- Shared acceptance criteria and specs

sightseeings.title: Reporting need of fixes with fixes
sightseeings.summary: A PR with tests wins over a report.
sightseeings.details:
- Pairing on a fix (with humans/agents) and delivering PRs with tests and fixes as reports
- Modern change advisory boards teach rules to distribute decisions

sightseeings.title: Task expansion
sightseeings.summary: We should probably discuss all these.
sightseeings.details:
- from low code tools to git first way of working
- from reporting to fixing
- from reviewing requirements through transforming to test cases to active contributions in context engineering
- from functional to visual and parafunctional
- from trustworthy specs to generated specs with need of alignment
- from separating test design and automation to combining them into continuous test automation
- from verifying against specs to verifying against alignment for resultsful testing (seeing problems that needs seeing)
- from individual contributor flows to team collaboration

sightseeings.title: Developers vs. knowledge workers
sightseeings.summary: Accepting different constraints
sightseeings.details: - Showing developer box contents

sightseeings.title: Reporting bugs without going through test cases

title: Coverage
summary: It's more than risks, requirements or code.

sightseeings.title: Risks, requirements and code
sightseeings.summary: The foundational coverage.
sightseeings.details:
- Risk-based coverage: identify what can go wrong, test that first
- Requirements-based coverage: every requirement traceable to at least one test
- Code coverage: line, branch, path — what each metric does and doesn't tell you
- The coverage illusion: 100% code coverage does not mean no bugs
- Combining dimensions: covering risks that never appeared in requirements
- Coverage gaps are explicit decisions, not failures to disclose

sightseeings.title: Quality practices agreement coverage
sightseeings.summary: Your adherence to agreements
sightseeings.details:
- Definition of done as a testable, agreed checklist
- Team agreements on coding standards, review checklists, branching rules
- Process coverage: did the team follow its own agreed practices?
- Automated policy checks: linting, security scanning, license compliance in CI
- Retrospective-driven improvement: agreements evolve as teams learn
- Difference between covering the product and covering how the team works

sightseeings.title: Coverage for the AI era
sightseeings.summary: Code reality and telemetry.
sightseeings.details:
- Code reality coverage: what the code actually does vs. what specs describe
- Telemetry-driven coverage: real user paths inform which tests matter
- Model behavior coverage: edge cases, adversarial inputs, output drift
- Prompt coverage: testing the space of inputs an AI system needs to handle
- Shadow testing and canary releases as live coverage signals
- Observability as continuous coverage running in production

sightseeings.title: Vibe coding exploratory testing coverage visualization

title: Resultsful exploratory testing
summary: Information is what we do testing for.

sightseeings.title: Seeing problems and conversation starters
sightseeings.summary: Results coverage of examples of risks realized matters.
sightseeings.details:
- See problems in simple targets
- Consistently using any tools necessary as constraints that enable your focus

sightseeings.title: Stop-the-line mentality
sightseeings.summary: Culture change for making bugs more worth attention
sightseeings.details:
- Andon cord: anyone can and should stop work when quality fails
- The cost of not stopping: defects compound and become more expensive downstream
- Psychological safety as a precondition — stopping the line cannot be punished
- When to escalate vs. when to fix and move on
- Low-friction, no-blame bug channels make bugs worth reporting
- Blameless post-mortems: learning from escapes rather than assigning fault

sightseeings.title: Deliberate practice of 4x Intent
sightseeings.summary: Tracking mission, charter, details and future.
sightseeings.details:
- the A4 practice tool
- AI adaptations of note taking

sightseeings.title: Strong-style navigation
sightseeings.summary: Intentionality in navigation from ensemble working
sightseeings.details:
- Strong-style navigation with people and agents
- Improving your intent
- Descriptive and prescriptive

title: Contemporary exploratory testing
summary: Things are different for exploratory testing.

sightseeings.title: Documenting with automation
sightseeings.summary: Automation in frame of exploratory testing
sightseeings.details:
- Grow what developers built
- Four purposes of programmatic tests for exploring

sightseeings.title: Agency for decisions
sightseeings.summary: Grow your decision-making muscle
sightseeings.details:
- Decisions about what to report
- Decisions about what to do first
- Decisions about what to leave out

title: Artifact-centric AI for testing
summary: Evolving artifacts keep the work visible and allow for sharing

sightseeings.title: Generating test ideas
sightseeings.summary: AI as external imagination
sightseeings.details:
- Prompt patterns for idea generation: persona, domain, risk angle
- AI as external imagination to overcome tester blind spots
- Mind map generation: AI as starting point, human as curator
- Category coverage: functional, edge cases, negative, security, performance
- Context injection: give AI the spec, get diverse test angles back
- Iterating on ideas: steering for quality over quantity

sightseeings.title: Generating test cases
sightseeings.summary: If you really need to, better be efficient about it
sightseeings.details:
- From test ideas to concrete cases with steps and expected results
- Structuring prompts for reproducible, specific test cases
- Keeping humans in the loop: reviewing generated cases for domain relevance
- Deduplication and coverage analysis of a generated set
- Parameterization: generating data tables alongside test logic
- When NOT to generate: cases requiring deep domain knowledge benefit from human design

sightseeings.title: Generating test automation
sightseeings.summary: Special tips for automation
sightseeings.details: - Automation first -approach

sightseeings.title: Evolving artifacts
sightseeings.summary: What you really want to do is evolving artifacts.
sightseeings.details:
- Testability in test targets
- Test ideas with risk and empirical setup
- Historical records
- Repeatability assets, particularly pipelines and test automation
- Information to react on, bugs, change requests, conversation starters
- Test coverage outlines
- Information to intake, questions, things that make work harder
- Test data
- Test tools
- Test strategy as ideas that guide our test design
- Test infrastructure and practices
- Test estimation
- Test narrative, of testing done so far
- Stakeholder relations
- Visibility, reporting and chartering forward

sightseeings.title: Requirements-based testing
sightseeings.summary: Test the ATM
sightseeings.details:
- ATM exercise: derive a test set from ATM requirements
- Identify: functional requirements, business rules, constraints
- Derive: positive tests, negative tests, boundary and edge case tests
- Map requirements to test cases for traceability
- Find: missing requirements, ambiguous requirements, conflicting requirements
- Deliver: a coverage outline with explicit gap analysis

title: Performance-centric AI for testing
summary: You don't know where the bugs are but you are systematic in finding out!

sightseeings.title: Playwright and Vibium as eyes/hands on browser
sightseeings.summary: AI can act for you, like you would
sightseeings.details:
- Playwright as browser automation: navigation, clicks, form fills, assertions
- Playwright MCP: giving AI agents real browser eyes and hands
- Vibium: visual AI for screenshot comparison and UI layout verification
- Combining programmatic and visual assertions for richer coverage
- Recording and replaying user flows as a starting scaffold
- Handling dynamic content: wait strategies and flakiness mitigation

sightseeings.title: CGI Test Intelligence Mesh
sightseeings.summary: Plugins of agents and skills for testing
sightseeings.details:
- The mesh concept: composable agents and skills for quality engineering
- Plugins for test generation, risk analysis, and coverage reporting
- Connecting tools: Playwright, Bruno, Jira, GitHub, monitoring systems
- Custom skills for domain-specific testing tasks
- Orchestrating mesh agents inside pipelines for autonomous quality gates
- Extending the mesh: contributing new skills to the shared library

sightseeings.title: Task expansion in practice
sightseeings.summary: Test and fix potion shop
sightseeings.details:
- Potion shop exercise: test and fix a known buggy application end-to-end
- From finding a bug to raising a PR with both the fix and a locking test
- Using agents to parallelize test execution across areas
- Measuring task expansion: what a single human+agent team can actually achieve
- Reflection: what required human judgment vs. what the agent handled autonomously

sightseeings.title: Agent-ready Test Professional
sightseeings.summary: CGI certifications for the modern age
sightseeings.details: Hire with 20 agents!

title: Vibe coding test visualizations
summary: Programming is a superpower for you

sightseeings.title: Intent to build apps
sightseeings.summary: Wish from the generator, does your wish bring about useful?
sightseeings.details:
- Starting from a wish: what problem does this tool actually solve?
- Describing intent well enough for AI to generate a useful scaffold
- Understanding the gap between 'it runs' and 'it is useful'
- Iterating on generated code: what to accept, what to redirect, what to discard
- From prototype to something you would actually share with a colleague
- Recognizing when vibe coding is the wrong tool and you need a real spec

sightseeings.title: Vibe coding a UI with local storage hosted on pages
sightseeings.summary: Experience of code with pipeline to app online
sightseeings.details: - Security considerations when building apps

sightseeings.title: From vibe coding to AI native development
sightseeings.summary: Spec-driven frameworks
sightseeings.details:
- Vibe coding: iterating with natural language until it works, fast but fragile
- AI native development: spec-driven, context-engineered, agent-executed
- Spec-driven frameworks: requirements as the primary source of truth
- CLAUDE.md and project context files as first-class engineering artifacts
- The human role in AI native workflows: direction, judgment, and verification
- Quality considerations: testing AI-generated code needs the same rigor as hand-written code

title: Architecture-awareness
summary: Modern testing must optimize and target.

sightseeings.title: Layered architectures and testability
sightseeings.summary: Understanding test strategy across architecture layers
sightseeings.details:
- Presentation, business logic, data layers and their seams
- Testing at each layer: UI, API, service, unit — and the trade-offs
- Dependency injection and testability design: seams enable control
- Contract testing between layers to catch integration breaks early
- Where mocks help and where they mask real integration failures
- Architecture diagrams as test strategy maps: risk lives at the boundaries

sightseeings.title: Cloud as someone else's computer
sightseeings.summary: With someone else's rules and defaults
sightseeings.details:
- Shared responsibility model: what the cloud provider owns vs. what you own
- Default configurations are often insecure — know what you inherit
- IAM, roles, and least-privilege: test environments need their own identities
- Cloud-specific failure modes: rate limits, regional outages, eventual consistency
- Infrastructure as code: your infrastructure config is also testable
- Cost awareness: testing in the cloud has a bill attached

sightseeings.title: Ephemeral test environments
sightseeings.summary: Setting up a separate test for every change of relevance
sightseeings.details:
- One environment per PR: containers, feature flags, or full stacks spun per branch
- Benefits: no test pollution between changes, parallel testing, always clean state
- GitHub Actions and ArgoCD for automated environment lifecycle management
- Docker Compose for local ephemeral stacks
- Cost management: environments that spin up on push and down on merge
- Data seeding strategies: how to get useful state in a fresh environment fast

sightseeings.title: Engineering environments with data
sightseeings.summary: Stop with the test environment, start with the shared environment.
sightseeings.details:
- Why 'test environment' is a dangerous mental model — it implies isolation that rarely exists
- Environments as a spectrum: local, PR, integration, staging, production
- Shared environments and data conflicts: test isolation as a first-class concern
- Synthetic data generation for realistic but safe test data
- Data masking and anonymization for production-like data in lower environments
- Environment parity: closing the gap between staging and production is a quality strategy

sightseeings.title: Testing systems with AI
sightseeings.summary: What if LLMs make up a part of your system?
sightseeings.details:
- LLM-based systems: non-determinism means same input can yield different output
- Testing for correctness, safety, alignment, and behavior under adversarial inputs
- Evaluation frameworks: LLM-as-judge, golden datasets, human review loops
- Regression testing for model updates and prompt changes
- Prompt injection and context poisoning as testable security risks
- Monitoring production AI behavior as a continuous form of testing

sightseeings.title: Architecture-awareness and lifecycle view
sightseeings.summary: Test the rabbit
sightseeings.details:
- Rabbit exercise: map the architecture of a given system from the outside in
- Identify: components, integrations, data flows, trust boundaries
- Derive: risk areas and testing priorities from the architecture map
- Lifecycle view: testing across build, deploy, run, and retire phases
- From architecture diagram to test strategy in one conversation
- Spotting testability gaps: what the architecture makes hard to test

sightseeings.title: Frontend fundamentals
sightseeings.summary: Knowing dev tools, html, css and the sorts.
sightseeings.details:
- HTML, CSS, typescript/javascript
- Dev tools
- SSR vs. CSR
- Caching, asynch

sightseeings.title: Backend Fundamentals
sightseeings.summary: REST and others
sightseeings.details:
- REST APIs: methods, status codes, headers, authentication patterns
- GraphQL: queries, mutations, schema — different testing surface than REST
- gRPC and asynchronous messaging: queues, events, and eventual consistency
- Databases: SQL basics, query patterns, transactions, and isolation levels
- Authentication: OAuth2, JWT, API keys — how to test each without hardcoding credentials
- API contracts and versioning: catching breaking changes before they break consumers

sightseeings.title: Mobile and Windows, Embedded Applications
sightseeings.summary: It's not all web.
sightseeings.details:
- Mobile testing: iOS, Android, device fragmentation, OS upgrade risk
- Mobile-specific risks: network conditions, battery, permissions, background behavior
- Windows application testing: WinUI, legacy Win32, Electron-wrapped web apps
- Embedded systems: limited resources, hardware dependencies, deterministic timing
- Appium and platform-specific frameworks for automation
- Simulators vs. real devices: what each can and cannot tell you

sightseeings.title: Generating fake data
sightseeings.summary: When you know where things live, you can control it.

title: Sociotechnical systems awareness
summary: Modeling the world as a core skill.

sightseeings.title: Systems thinking tools
sightseeings.summary: Modeling systems to focus thinking
sightseeings.details:
- DSPR
- More testers, worse quality
- Signals and levers

sightseeings.title: Making lightweight plans
sightseeings.summary: Concise plans, and working with backlogs for testing.
sightseeings.details:
- One-page test strategy: what, why, scope, risks, approach — nothing more
- Working with backlogs: test tasks as user stories with acceptance criteria
- Just-enough planning: enough to start, flexible enough to adapt as you learn
- Risk registers as living test plans: update them as the system changes
- Timeboxed planning: how much plan is proportionate to how much work
- Signals and levers: identifying what you can actually change vs. what you report on

sightseeings.title: Mandate levels
sightseeings.summary: Modeling mandate levels

sightseeings.title: Risks awareness
sightseeings.summary: Targeting efforts based on risks

title: Observability
summary: Telemetry, logs, metrics and all that.

sightseeings.title: Telemetry
sightseeings.summary: Sending information about use, test vs. production
sightseeings.details:
- What telemetry is: events, traces, metrics, logs — and how they differ
- Sending telemetry from test runs vs. from production users
- OpenTelemetry as the standard protocol for instrumentation
- Separating test traffic from real user traffic in shared dashboards
- Using production telemetry to validate that features are actually used
- Alerting on telemetry anomalies as a quality signal

sightseeings.title: Visualizing real-time status
sightseeings.summary: Make it visual - builds and systems
sightseeings.details:
- Build dashboards: pipeline health, pass/fail rates, flaky test trends over time
- System dashboards: uptime, error rates, latency percentiles
- Grafana, DataDog, and cloud-native monitoring as quality tools
- Team radiators: always-visible quality signals on a shared screen
- Connecting test results to monitoring dashboards for a unified view
- Alert fatigue: fewer, higher-signal alerts beat many low-value ones

sightseeings.title: Token optimization and FinOps
sightseeings.summary: Seeing what AI costs for value generation
sightseeings.details:
- Token counting: input, output, and context window costs are all billable
- Prompt caching and its impact on cost at scale
- Cost per test run with AI-assisted automation: is the value worth the spend?
- FinOps principles: visibility first, then optimization, then accountability
- Setting cost budgets for AI testing tools at team and project level
- Balancing AI quality gains against token spend — not all uses are worth it

sightseeings.title: Performance, security and reliability
sightseeings.summary: Know the system scale features
sightseeings.details:
- Performance testing types: load (normal), stress (peak), soak (sustained), spike (sudden)
- Security testing basics: OWASP Top 10, threat modeling, attack surface analysis
- Reliability: MTTR, MTBF, SLAs and SLOs as testable acceptance criteria
- Chaos engineering: deliberately breaking systems to find weaknesses before users do
- Shift-left for non-functional requirements: define them before build, not after
- Parafunctional requirements written as acceptance criteria the same as functional ones

sightseeings.title: Usability and accessibility
sightseeings.summary: Know the friction and compliance
sightseeings.details:
- Usability heuristics: Nielsen's 10 principles as a lightweight review checklist
- Cognitive load and friction: where users slow down or give up
- Accessibility standards: WCAG 2.1 AA as the baseline compliance target
- Screen readers, keyboard navigation, color contrast as concrete test dimensions
- Automated scanning finds about 30% of accessibility issues — manual testing covers the rest
- Inclusive design: designing for extremes serves everyone better

sightseeings.title: Financial and legal
sightseeings.summary: Know the value and risks
sightseeings.details:
- Financial risk: billing bugs, rounding errors, currency handling, negative amounts
- Regulatory compliance: GDPR, PCI-DSS, SOC2, ISO 27001 as testing scope drivers
- License compliance: open source obligations that arise from your component choices
- Audit trails: what the system must log to satisfy legal and contractual obligations
- Contract testing: does the system actually meet its stated legal commitments?
- Risk quantification: translating defects into financial exposure to drive prioritization

title: Process, practice and collaboration
summary: Logistics of when to show up with information

sightseeings.title: Agile, incremental, waterfall
sightseeings.summary: How process shapes when and how testing happens
sightseeings.details:
- Waterfall: testing as a phase, handoff model, late and expensive feedback loops
- Agile/Scrum: testing within sprints, definition of done as the quality gate
- Continuous delivery: testing embedded in the pipeline, not scheduled after it
- Incremental delivery: testing each increment independently before building the next
- Context-dependent: no single process fits all teams, products, or risk profiles
- The tester's role and leverage shifts significantly with each process model

sightseeings.title: Retrospectives and test improvement
sightseeings.summary: Using reflection to improve quality practices
sightseeings.details:
- Retrospective formats: 4Ls, Start/Stop/Continue, timeline — pick the one that fits
- Identifying quality-related impediments as first-class retro topics
- Tracking escaped defects and near-misses as improvement fuel
- Setting measurable improvement goals: not 'test more' but 'reduce escape rate by X'
- Quality metrics to review: defect escape rate, cycle time, test coverage, flaky test count
- Blameless culture as a precondition for honest retrospectives on quality failures

sightseeings.title: Examples (TDD, BDD)  and experiments
sightseeings.summary: Using examples to drive development and validate behavior
sightseeings.details:
- TDD: test-first development, red-green-refactor — the test defines the contract
- BDD: behavior-driven development, Gherkin syntax, specifications as living documentation
- Example mapping: structured conversation to discover scenarios before writing code
- Examples as shared understanding tools, not just test scripts
- Experiments: hypothesis, measure, learn loop applied to testing decisions
- When TDD/BDD pays off and when it is overhead: context determines value

sightseeings.title: Intent lead
sightseeings.summary: Decides what must be learned, protected, proven, or observed.
sightseeings.details:
- Define quality outcomes, not just features
- Align acceptance criteria with measurable business value
- Prioritize risks: regulatory, security, reputation, usability, AI hallucination
- Define “done” beyond functionality: reliable, explainable, observable, resilient
- Drive production quality KPIs: defect escape rate, customer friction, AI response accuracy, trust score, SLA/SLO adherence

sightseeings.title: Value engineer
sightseeings.summary: Continuously experiments to discover whether the system creates value safely and effectively.
sightseeings.details:
- Create continuous evaluation frameworks
- Validate: correctness, usefulness, consistency, personalization quality
- Run: A/B experiments, telemetry analysis. behavior-driven evaluations
- Establish “golden datasets”
- Monitor production drift: model drift. response variance, performance degradation
- Quantify business impact: reduced effort, accuracy improvements, faster decisions

sightseeings.title: Context engineer
sightseeings.summary: Creates the shared understanding that makes meaningful testing possible.
sightseeings.details:
- Engineer high-quality prompts and grounding data
- Create testable AI contexts
- Validate: prompt consistency, retrieval quality, context relevance, memory/window limitations
- Design synthetic and edge-case datasets
- Prevent: context poisoning, prompt injection, stale knowledge
- Build traceability between: requirements, prompts, outputs, evaluations

sightseeings.title: Alignment engineer
sightseeings.summary: Ensures the system stays coherent, trustworthy, and directionally correct.
sightseeings.details:
- Verify AI behavior aligns with: policies, regulations, ethical constraints, brand standards
- Conduct adversarial testing: jailbreak attempts, prompt injection, malicious inputs
- Validate: fairness, explainability, auditability, transparency
- Define trust controls: human-in-the-loop, approval workflows, risk thresholds
- Ensure AI observability and traceability

sightseeings.title: Experience engineer
sightseeings.summary: Makes system quality observable, understandable, and actionable for humans.
sightseeings.details:
- Validate conversational quality
- Ensure: clarity, cognitive simplicity, accessibility, responsiveness
- Test multi-turn interactions
- Validate confidence indicators and fallback behavior
- Assess human trust and adoption
- Ensure graceful degradation when AI confidence is low

sightseeings.title: WebUI: Playwright
sightseeings.summary: Must know one tool.
sightseeings.details:
- Playwright
- Playwright MCP and CLI
- Playwright agents and skills
- Selenium BiDi and architecture-awareness on CDP
- Vibium

sightseeings.title: APIs: Bruno and Pytest + Requests
sightseeings.summary: Must know a low code and code tool.
sightseeings.details:
- Bruno
- Pytest + Requests

sightseeings.title: AI Harness: Github Copilot
sightseeings.summary: Must know one harness.
sightseeings.details:
- Github Copilot
- Planning mode, Agentic mode
- Agents and skills
- Options: Claude Code, Codex, Kiro

sightseeings.title: Key MCPs: Playwright, Rovo, Figma
sightseeings.summary: Model Context Protocol plugins that extend AI testing capabilities
sightseeings.details:
- MCP: the protocol for giving AI agents structured access to tools and context
- Playwright MCP: browser automation and visual inspection available to agents
- Rovo MCP: Atlassian ecosystem integration — Jira issues, Confluence pages as agent context
- Figma MCP: design spec access for visual regression and design-to-implementation testing
- Composing MCPs: combining multiple tools in a single agent session
- Writing your own MCP: packaging a custom tool as an agent-callable plugin

sightseeings.title: Diagrams: Mermaid
sightseeings.summary: Drawing pictures that are cheaper to read.
sightseeings.details:
- Mermaid diagrams
- Archimate
- Generating documentation from code

sightseeings.title: Formatted documents: Markdown
sightseeings.summary: From Confluence and Jira to Git First
sightseeings.details:
- Confluence and Jira (Rovo) MCP
- Publishing flows

sightseeings.title: Local framework: Robot Framework
sightseeings.summary: I guess this is worth mention.
sightseeings.details:
- RF is language, and framework with unified reports
- Smaller community, worse AI experience

sightseeings.title: Repo hosting services: Github, Gitlab, ADO
sightseeings.summary: Where code lives and collaboration happens
sightseeings.details:
- GitHub: Issues, Actions, Pages, Codespaces, Copilot — the dominant ecosystem
- GitLab: built-in CI/CD pipelines, container registry, environments in one platform
- Azure DevOps (ADO): Boards, Pipelines, Repos, Test Plans for Microsoft-stack clients
- Choosing based on ecosystem: what the client already uses often decides the answer
- Cross-platform skill: git is universal, the hosting wrapper varies — learn both layers
- Security controls: branch protection, CODEOWNERS, required reviews, signed commits

sightseeings.title: CI/CD: Jenkins, Github Actions, ADO
sightseeings.summary: Automating the path from commit to production
sightseeings.details:
- Continuous integration: every push triggers build and tests — fast feedback
- Continuous delivery/deployment: automated, reliable path from commit to production
- Jenkins: server-based, plugin-heavy, Jenkinsfile declarative pipelines
- GitHub Actions: workflow YAML, marketplace actions, matrix builds, runner types
- ADO Pipelines: YAML or classic editor, tight Azure integration
- Key testing integration points: test result publishing, artifact storage, failure notifications

sightseeings.title: Local 3rd party components
sightseeings.summary: Seeing the lego blocks of the world
sightseeings.details:
- Memory
- Compression before LLMs

sightseeings.title: Accessibility: Lighthouse and aXe
sightseeings.summary: Automated tooling for accessibility coverage
sightseeings.details:
- Lighthouse: automated scoring for accessibility, performance, SEO, and best practices
- aXe: WCAG-mapped rule engine available as browser extension and as an API
- Integration with Playwright: axe-playwright for accessibility checks in CI
- Interpreting results: which violations are critical vs. advisory, and how to triage
- Manual checks that automation cannot replace: focus management, screen reader flow, meaningful alt text
- Accessibility as a code quality signal: bad accessibility often points to bad structure

sightseeings.title: Security:  Threat modeling and Burbsuite
sightseeings.summary: Structured thinking and active tooling for security testing
sightseeings.details:
- Threat modeling: STRIDE framework, attack trees, data flow diagrams
- Identifying trust boundaries and threat actors before writing a test
- Burp Suite: intercepting proxy, active scanner, repeater for manual web app testing
- OWASP ZAP as an open source alternative with CI integration
- Common web vulnerabilities to test: injection, broken auth, IDOR, XSS, CSRF
- Security testing in CI: DAST for running apps, SAST for code scanning

sightseeings.title: Performance: Jmeter and cloud scaling
sightseeings.summary: Measuring system behavior under load
sightseeings.details:
- JMeter: test plans, thread groups, samplers, listeners — the core concepts
- Performance metrics: throughput, latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99), error rate
- Cloud-based load generation: scaling beyond what a single machine can produce
- Interpreting results: finding the breaking point vs. the safe operating envelope
- Performance budgets: acceptable thresholds defined as acceptance criteria upfront
- Cloud auto-scaling: test both the application and the scaling behavior under load

sightseeings.title: Certified Modern Testing Talent
sightseeings.summary: CGI certifications for the modern age
sightseeings.details:
- CGI certification path for the AI era of quality engineering
- Skills assessed: context engineering, agent orchestration, quality leadership
- Practical demonstration over written examination: show, don't just describe
- Portfolio as evidence: PRs, pipelines, test strategies, and tools built
- Renewal cadence: keeping pace with tooling evolution in the AI era
- Recognition: internal mobility and client credibility backed by demonstrated skill

title: Shipping tested production code autonomously
summary: Dark factory pattern and related test practices

sightseeings.title: From reactive agents to proactive agents and dark factories
sightseeings.summary: Risk-based human in the loop
sightseeings.details:
- Risk-based categorization
- Working on PRs and ephemeral test environments
- Orchestrating quality engineering agents to dark factories
- Vuln patching factory

sightseeings.title: Property-based testing
sightseeings.summary: Generating tests against properties
sightseeings.details:
- Generate many random inputs automatically and let the framework find edge cases
- A property is an invariant that must always hold, not a specific example
- Hypothesis (Python) and fast-check (JavaScript/TypeScript) as primary frameworks
- Shrinking: the framework finds the minimal failing input automatically
- Combining with AI: generating domain-relevant custom generators
- Best fit: pure functions, data transformations, protocol compliance, encoding round-trips

sightseeings.title: Mutation testing
sightseeings.summary: Testing the tests
sightseeings.details:
- A mutant is a code change that should cause at least one test to fail
- Mutation score: what percentage of mutants did the test suite kill?
- Tools: Pitest (Java), Mutmut (Python), Stryker (JS/TS)
- The key insight: code coverage lets many mutants survive; mutation testing catches them
- Practical use: find weak assertions, missing branches, and under-tested logic
- Cost: computationally expensive — run on critical or high-risk modules, not everything
